history.content:
<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 08 column G. Moving forward would hit lava at row 02 column C, so I should turn to avoid it. I’ll turn down (`v`) by turning right from facing right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Now I’m at row 02 column B facing down (`v`). Forward would go to row 03 column B, which is a safe floor (`.`). I’ll move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column B facing down (`v`). Forward would move to row 04 column B, which is a safe floor (`.`). I’ll move forward to continue descending toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 04 column B facing down (`v`). Forward would go to row 05 column B, which is safe floor (`.`). I’ll move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 05 column B facing down (`v`). Forward would move to row 06 column B, which is a safe floor (`.`). I’ll move forward to keep progressing.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 06 column B facing down (`v`). Forward goes to row 07 column B, which is lava (`L`), so it’s unsafe. I should turn to avoid lava; turning right would make me face left (`<`) relative to map? Actually from `v`, TURN_RIGHT faces left (`<`?)—on a compass, down->left for TURN_RIGHT. Left cell is row 06 column A which is `#` (wall). TURN_LEFT from down faces right (`>`) which would move to row 06 column C, also `.`. I’ll turn left to face right and proceed.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 06 column B facing right (`>`). Forward would move to row 06 column C, which is safe floor (`.`). I’ll move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 06 column C facing right (`>`). Forward would move to row 06 column D, which is `.` and safe. I should move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 06 column D facing right (`>`). Forward would move to row 06 column E, which is `.` and safe. I’ll move forward to progress toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 06 column E facing right (`>`). Forward would move to row 06 column F, which is `.` and safe. I should move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 06 column F facing right (`>`). Forward would move to row 06 column G, which is `.` and safe. Continue toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 06 column G facing right (`>`). Forward would move to row 06 column H, which is `.` and safe (not lava or wall). Move forward toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 06 column H facing right (`>`). Forward would move to row 06 column I, which is `.`? Actually column I is '.' and within the open area; the goal at row 08 column H is downwards, but I should keep heading to align with column H. Instead, I should turn left to head down toward the corridor while staying in column H.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m now at row 06 column H facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 08 column H, directly two cells below. So I need to face down (`v`) to move toward it. From up to down, I should turn right twice, but one step at a time: first TURN_RIGHT.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 06 column H facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 08 column H, so I need to face down (`v`). From right to down is a 90° clockwise turn, so I will TURN_RIGHT.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 06 column H facing down (`v`). The cell directly in front is row 07 column H, which is floor `.` (not lava `L` and not wall `#`). I should MOVE_FORWARD to get closer to the goal at row 08 column H.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column H facing down (`v`). The cell in front (row 08 column H) is `G`, the goal. I should MOVE_FORWARD to reach it.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
